title: Board of Peace Gaza Disarmament Plan Revealed: Full Hamas Disarmament Required by Day 90
detail: Details of the Mladenov disarmament plan became public. The eight-month framework requires full disarmament of Hamas and all Palestinian armed factions — including personal firearms — and destruction of tunnel networks by Day 90. Phase 1 (Days 1–14): full military cessation and humanitarian protocols. Phase 2 (Days 16–90): Hamas removes heavy weapons from Israeli-controlled areas, then all areas. Phase 3 (Day 90+): IDF withdraws to Gaza perimeters after monitoring confirms full disarmament (by Day 251). Reconstruction — including lifting 'dual-use' restrictions on concrete, steel, and fuel — is conditioned on verified disarmament compliance; non-compliant zones to be treated as 'rogue areas.' Hamas responded with an immediate and categorical refusal: 'We will not give up arms as long as occupation continues.' Al Jazeera reported the US is effectively seeking 'Hamas political surrender.' Approximately $7 billion in reconstruction pledges had already been collected, primarily from Gulf states. Tenders were also issued for construction of 'New Rafah' — a 'humanitarian city' east of the Yellow Line — raising international alarm about coerced Palestinian relocation in violation of international law.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Details revealed of Board of Peace plan for Gaza disarmament, 26 March 2026
Al Jazeera — US seeks Hamas political surrender in new Gaza plan, 26 March 2026

title: IDF Chief of Staff Zamir: '10 Red Flags Before the IDF Collapses' — Forces Stretched Across Five Fronts
detail: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned the security cabinet of critical manpower shortages: 'I am raising 10 red flags before the IDF collapses into itself.' The IDF was simultaneously engaged across the Iran front, Lebanon (Hezbollah), Syria, West Bank, and Gaza. Zamir disclosed that Lebanon invasion forces had been diverted to the West Bank to contain the surge in organized settler violence — characterizing the attacks against Palestinians as 'morally and ethically unacceptable' and a threat to military cohesion. The warning underscored the unsustainability of operating across five simultaneous fronts while managing internal settler violence.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — IDF forces diverted to West Bank as chief warns of sustainability crisis, 26 March 2026
Haaretz — Zamir warns cabinet: I am raising 10 red flags before the IDF collapses, 26 March 2026

title: 15 Diplomatic Missions Condemn 'Settler Terror'; 257 Incidents Recorded in 25 Days Since Feb 28
detail: Fifteen diplomatic missions including EU member states, Canada, and the UK condemned organized settler violence as 'settler terror' in unusually direct terms — described by B'Tselem's Sarit Michaeli as unprecedented in its directness from Western governments. Christian Science Monitor reported 257 incidents of settler violence or harassment in the West Bank in the first 25 days after February 28. Six Palestinians were shot and killed by settlers in that period. Yesh Din found only 3% of settler violence cases since October 2023 resulted in Israeli convictions. The UN Security Council held its scheduled briefing on 'The Middle East, including the Palestinian Question,' with the Deputy Special Coordinator calling on parties to consolidate the Gaza ceasefire and halt escalating settlement activity.

sources.name: Christian Science Monitor — Israeli settler violence condemned by 15 diplomatic missions, 26 March 2026
UN Security Council — Briefing on the Palestinian Question (SC16290), 26 March 2026
Yesh Din — Settler violence accountability data, 2025
